title: The Future of ATM
author: Charles T. Robbins (under Tom Willmott)
thesis_summary: ATM (Asynchronous Transfer Mode) would become the universal networking backbone and desktop standard, displacing Ethernet, FDDI, and Token Ring; converged transport for voice, video, and data.
recovery_notes: Founding artifact of Aberdeen's 1991-1997 networking thesis. Set the pin for ATM coverage for ~4 years. Recovered 1995-2000 studies document the shift away, not the origin. Check Wayback Machine, ex-Aberdeen colleagues, library archives.

title: The Future of Transaction Processing
author: Peter S. Kastner (ghostwritten for John Logan)
thesis_summary: Future of transaction processing; specifics pending recovery. Authored by Peter S. Kastner but published under John Logan's name at Yankee Group.
recovery_notes: Yankee Group publication, not Aberdeen. Logged here because Kastner is the ghostwriter and the study is part of his analyst corpus. Recovery would require Yankee Group archives or Kastner's personal records.
